2604.11801.title: CLSGen: A Dual-Head Fine-Tuning Framework for Joint Probabilistic Classification and Verbalized Explanation
2604.11801.paper_summary: With the recent progress of Large Language Models (LLMs), there is a growing interest in applying these models to solve complex and challenging problems. Modern LLMs, capable of processing long contexts and generating verbalized explanations, offer significant potential in addressing real-world applications. However, a critical hurdle in deploying LLMs for practical decision-making is their inability to provide reliable, quantitative probabilities. While task-specific fine-tuning of LLMs using traditional discriminative objectives (similar to encoder-only models) can yield probability estimates, this often leads to catastrophic forgetting and linguistic collapse. Consequently, the model loses its ability to generate explanations, severely undermining its interpretability and usability. To address this challenge, we propose CLSGen, a novel LLM fine-tuning framework designed for binary classification tasks. The CLSGen framework encompasses a new model architecture, training methodology, and data construction strategy to enable robust probability estimation without sacrificing the model's inherent explanation-generation capabilities. Experimental results across multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that models fine-tuned with CLSGen outperform existing baselines in classification metrics (AUROC and F1-score). Regarding explanation, the results showed strong alignment between predicted labels and generated justifications, as well as high readability.
2604.11801.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型（LLMs）的近期進展，對於將這些模型應用於解決複雜和具挑戰性問題的興趣日益增加。現代LLMs能夠處理長上下文並生成口頭解釋，在解決現實世界應用方面具有重要潛力。然而，部署LLMs進行實際決策的一個關鍵障礙是它們無法提供可靠的定量概率。雖然使用傳統的判別目標對LLMs進行特定任務的微調（類似於僅編碼器模型）可以產生概率估計，但這往往導致災難性遺忘和語言崩潰。因此，模型失去了生成解釋的能力，嚴重削弱了其可解釋性和可用性。為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了CLSGen，一個針對二元分類任務設計的新型LLM微調框架。CLSGen框架包含了一種新的模型架構、訓練方法和數據構建策略，以實現穩健的概率估計，而不犧牲模型固有的解釋生成能力。多個基準數據集的實驗結果顯示，使用CLSGen微調的模型在分類指標（AUROC和F1-score）上超越了現有基準。關於解釋，結果顯示預測標籤與生成的理由之間有很強的一致性，以及高可讀性。
2604.11801.author: WonJin Yoon et.al.
2604.11801.authors: WonJin Yoon, Kangyu Zhu, Ian Bulovic, Autumn Sehy, Yanjun Gao, Dmitriy Dligach, Majid Afshar, Timothy A. Miller
